title: Money Always Returns and Grows
subtitle: An affirmation for anchoring trust in money's continuous replenishment
excerpt: A brief affirmation for when the clinging pattern appears — the fear that money spent will not come back. The statement names money as something that always renews, and gives the nervous system a specific, present-tense alternative to brace-for-scarcity.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 61 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you catch yourself holding money too tightly without a specific reason, or on mornings when the sense of scarcity is there before any bill arrives.

content.text: A short affirmation for the clinging pattern. The specific fear underneath it is that money once gone stays gone — that the next dry spell is certain, that holding on is safer than trusting flow. The affirmation names a different fact: that money moves, returns, and grows.

The affirmation is most useful as a morning practice before the day builds, or at the moment you catch the clinging feeling appearing — the hand staying on the wallet, the delay on a sensible decision.
Money always returns and grows. Every spend is matched with new supply. I trust the cycle.

Say it out loud three times, hand on heart. The 'I trust the cycle' line is the load-bearing one — it shifts the frame from control to trust. Pair with the Day 61 tapping practice when the clinging feeling is persistent.
Adapted from the Day 61 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
